presentation.title: AI Workbench Local — Policy / Contract Comparison
presentation.subtitle: Executive comparison review of grounded document differences
presentation.author: AI Workbench Local

executive_summary:
The Access Review Evidence Log and the Privileged Account Approval Email address distinct control objectives: the former focuses on verifying the completeness and provenance of vendor access evidence for closure, while the latter governs conditional, time-bound privileged access with usage limits and mandatory logging for specific personnel.  

- Access Review Evidence Log requires closure of evidence items only after verifying provenance and completeness, with missing items blocking closure.  
- Privileged Account Approval Email mandates strict usage limits, mandatory logging, and quarterly vendor recertification for approved personnel.  
- Evidence Log is synthetic and intended for testing, not real-world audit use; Privileged Account Approval Email is an operational directive for live access management.  
- Evidence Log tracks five discrete evidence items with statuses and repository locations; Privileged Account Approval Email applies to two named administrators under a pilot program.  
- Control closure in Evidence Log depends on archival completeness; Privileged Account Approval Email requires ongoing compliance with logging and vendor validation.  
- Evidence Log addresses vendor access controls broadly; Privileged Account Approval Email targets specific personnel with scoped, temporary access.
key_highlights: The Access Review Evidence Log and the Privileged Account Approval Email address distinct control objectives: the former focuses on verifying the completeness and provenance of vendor access evidence for closure, while the latter governs conditional, time-bound privileged access with usage limits and mandatory logging for specific personnel.
Access Review Evidence Log requires closure of evidence items only after verifying provenance and completeness, with missing items blocking closure.
Privileged Account Approval Email mandates strict usage limits, mandatory logging, and quarterly vendor recertification for approved personnel.
Evidence Log is synthetic and intended for testing, not real-world audit use; Privileged Account Approval Email is an operational directive for live access management.
Evidence Log tracks five discrete evidence items with statuses and repository locations; Privileged Account Approval Email applies to two named administrators under a pilot program.
Control closure in Evidence Log depends on archival completeness; Privileged Account Approval Email requires ongoing compliance with logging and vendor validation.
